developer_response.body:
We are sorry to hear that Hand Physics Lab didn’t quite meet your expectations.

Regarding your experience, please note that switching between controllers and hand tracking is managed by the Meta Quest, rather than the game itself.

How to fix this: You can prevent accidental switching by going into your Meta Quest Settings and turning off the Auto-Switch feature between controllers and hand tracking.

We hope this improves your experience!

developer_response.body:
We are sorry Hand Physics Lab didn’t meet your expectations. Please note that the Hand tracking is provided by the Meta Quest, not Hand Physics Lab.
The quality of the hand tracking heavily depends on the lighting and other conditions. Try the following

- Have good lighting in the room
- No direct sunlight or glares from windows
- Clean the cameras on your Meta Quest with the same fabric you clean the lenses
- Good contrast between the color of your hands and the background
- Keep both hands in the field of view of the Meta Quest
- Try not to move too fast in general

We hope this helps!

reviews.review_description: this game is great! however, when i was playing with controllers, i noticed that controller mode doesn't seem to have a lot of... Flexibility... Another thing i want to ad is i want a sandbox mode where you can freely make and add objects in any environment. Otherwise, great game! Definitely a must have for newer VR players.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your feedback. In Hand Physics Lab, everything is physics based on purpose. That's the challenge of the puzzles. 

Here is a trick: if you go close to an object, a white ring with a line appears. This means that if you make a fist, the object gets snapped to your hand and stays until you open your fist.

reviews.review_description: .This game has the potential to be one of the most amazing vr experiences. Hand tracking is still fairly new and I believe the developers were too ambitious. I started playing this game on my brand new Oculus Quest 3 and after a playing for over and hour I was sorely disappointed. My issue stems from the hand tracking, it is complete garbage. There will be moments where I am holding onto an object in the game and I will open my hand fully and no matter what the hand in the game is still holding the object. When you try to move your hand away, most of the time the physics will bug out, either sending the object flying or even your hand will clip through the the table in front of you. And before you say “are the cameras on your Quest 3 clean?”. Not only are they cleaned regularly with a microfiber cloth, but as I mentioned before this is also a brand new vr headset so there is no way a hardware issue. This game is a buggy mess through and through. It seems like they never even play tested it. There are so many obvious improvements you could make that are so simple and would improve the gameplay exponentially. I truly hate to say it but this game is genuinely frustrating to play at times. There are far too many moments where your in game hands are not moving in a way that mimic your real life hands. This level of frustration makes this game almost unplayable for long periods of time. All of my friends and family that have played this game have had similar experiences where the hand tracking is just not working at intended. I very much hope that technology will advance to the point where hand tracking is near perfect and seamless, sadly this is just not even close and a complete let down.

reviews.review_description: As a tech demo, it has good novelty factors; however, there are baffling game design decisions that make this frustrating to play. For example, my in-game hands have physics collision with itself. This is not necessary because my real-life hands already have physics with the real world. When I lose hand tracking and bring them back into view, I always have a finger stuck in my palm or tangled with other fingers. Having physics and collision for in-game hands is finicky and unnecessary. Another example is the inertia of sliding bars in the options menu. I wanted to scale the size of my in-game hands to my real-life hands, but I couldn't because when I lifted my finger from the slide, the momentum of that action caused the slider to continue to move. Hand tracking is great and all but this tech demo has got it all wrong. Interactions are frustrating and cumbersome. At some point, the hand tracking will become better, but we are not there yet.

Whenever I do something like placing down blocks that have to match the same colour my arm gets in the way and it glitches every thing and the blocks will fly everywhere, and also the quest 2 hand tracking is so freaking bad which makes me so frustrated to the point that I cant play it anymore, so if you could fix something like for example when you place down a block and the correct spot, it should stay there so I can play this game better. Other than that good game!

reviews.review_description: I don’t like how you have to complete stages to get all of the playground levels, it’s pretty dumb which makes the game boring

Using hand tracking here is not precise and it has many issues, even is the best lit room. Trying to grab things, flip switches and everything else works a fraction of the time - the rest it's untidy infuriating. 

There doesn't seem to be an abundance of mini games, even a few hours in, so the only thing that changes as you go through is the difficulty of puzzles. 

It's interesting for those who want to experience hand tracking, but this game makes it seem more like a gimmick. There are better games using it at this point. 

If this wasn't free from Quest+ it would be a return heavy game.

developer_response.body:
We are sorry Hand Physics Lab didn’t meet your expectations. Please note that the Hand tracking is provided by the Meta Quest, not Hand Physics Lab.
The quality of the hand tracking not only depends on a good lit room. Try the following

- No direct sunlight or glares from windows
- Clean the cameras on your Meta Quest with the same fabric you clean the lenses
- Good contrast between the color of your hands and the background
- Keep both hands in the field of view of the Meta Quest
- Try not to move too fast in general

If you have an issue grabbing objects, do the following: Go close to an object with your hand until a white circle appears. Then, make a full fist to snap the object to your hand. 

We hope this helps!

reviews.review_description: This is the first time I’ve ever messed with hand tracking since I bought my Quest 3 and this game is a great introduction to this technology. It offers a wide variety of different types of puzzles and random VR activities to keep you busy and having fun. I like to find good games and experience that my 8yr old niece can enjoy. I’m a 41yr old man so the majority of the games I play are geared towards adult and use full locomotion. Not only are most of my games not appropriate for children but even if they were,it would cause her to get motion sickness. For these reasons I focus on getting stationary games that don’t require a lot of free movement and mixed reality games. So far,this has easily been her favorite VR game.she is literally sitting next to me in the game breaking glass with a hammer and talking about how much fun she is having lol
Not recommended

So sand box mode slaps!… for a week. There is just not enough sand box content. One thing that would make this game a gem is if you added player built maps. Please add this. And by the way if your having trouble with the big red BAD HAND TRACKING signs, there is a setting to turn it off. Let me tell you it is a game changer. Also I know that this might be, well impossible with hand tracking, but a open world mode where you can fire like big automatic nerf and/ or air soft guns that would be endless ours of fun.(make it controller only) also multiplayer at all if you add more updates would be fun two. Another thing is making physics gadgets. I don’t have sport mode but I’d imagine the gadgets in that game are good. That’s it! A response would be great. And I know developing is hard. So I won’t hold you by your neck for this not for sure update ideas. Do what you want with your game. It’s yours after all. (Edit: i am now checking every oculus notification I get for this update I’m exited to see what you have planned!)
Old review they haven’t updated in a year. I’d say it’s worth the move tho

Many of the negative reviews focus on bad hand tracking. While the hand tracking isn't perfect, I think it would be good enough if it wasn't for the real problem, bad design. Everything in the game SHOULD be playing to the strengths of its selling point, short levels having fun with hand tracking. But instead it feels like it was designed without even thinking about the weaknesses of hand tracking. And many times it felt like they mistook jank for an interesting challenge. To give a specific, those slide block puzzle levels? Everyone has played one of those before, adding hand tracking and physics jank just makes the execution annoying, which is not good for that type of puzzle. CHANGES I THINK WOULD HELP THE GAME: Hands should not have collision with each other, fingers too, it adds unnecessary jank. Make some objects lock to a certain spot when you grab them, clearly the tracking can't be trusted for those situations. Forearms aren't tracked, they are made up by the game, so they shouldn't put forces on the hands, and possibly shouldn't have collision until the wrist. Make the level stay when you finish it, if I built a tower I want to knock it down before continuing. That's about all I have to say, overall I would not recommend the game, even if you wanted to play with controllers, all that does is make the game more generic and boring.
I think this game is both fun and funny when I played the cat level I almost passed out laughing  I would recommend playing the game the game itself is a 10/10

This game was amazing for the first few hours then it started to get a little boring but a multiplayer game mode would be amazing just the same puzzles but with someone else or a race to do the most puzzles in like 5 minutes.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review and feedback. We would love to add multiplayer to Hand Physics Lab, but because everything is physics-based and because of the performance limitation of the Meta Quest, multiplayer is unfortunately impossible.
